projectInstructions: MANDATORY: Before writing ANY code on this project, read AGENT_ONBOARDING_CHECKLIST.md and follow it step by step. It contains the required reading order, verification steps, and the five most common mistakes to avoid. Then read BUILDER_FIELD_MANUAL.md — operational tradecraft that will save you debugging cycles. Key rules: (1) Use EF.* constants from aidm/schemas/entity_fields.py for all entity field access — never bare string literals. (2) This is D&D 3.5e — no 5e concepts (no advantage/disadvantage, no short/long rest, damage type is 'electricity' not 'electric'). (3) Resolvers return events only, never mutate WorldState. (4) No set() objects in state, no floats in deterministic paths, all json.dumps must use sort_keys=True. (5) Do NOT fix items in KNOWN_TECH_DEBT.md unless explicitly asked. After every CP: run 'python -m pytest tests/ -v --tb=short' (expect 1700+ tests passing in <10s) and update PROJECT_STATE_DIGEST.md. (6) When you complete a deliverable that needs PM review (work order output, design doc, spec, audit report), copy it to pm_inbox/ as a .md file named {AGENT}_{WO-id}_{short_description}.md (e.g. SONNET-C_WO-M1-01_event_reducer_impl.md). Include a header block with Agent, Work Order, Date, and Status fields. Thunder will drag-drop these to the PM. (7) If you notice something worth flagging (judgment calls, ambiguities, possible bugs outside your scope, pattern violations in nearby code), append an entry to pm_inbox/SONNET_AGENT_NOTES.md using the template in that file. (8) MANDATORY COMMIT RULE: After tests pass, write your debrief and update pm_inbox/PM_BRIEFING_CURRENT.md FIRST. Then 'git add' ALL files — code, tests, debrief, AND briefing update. Then 'git commit -m "feat: WO-XXX — short description"'. Then add the commit hash to your debrief header and 'git commit --amend --no-edit'. This ensures the debrief is committed with the code — not left as an untracked file. Everything in the working tree is at risk of loss if uncommitted. (9) DEBRIEF FORMAT: 500 words max. Four mandatory sections: Scope Accuracy (one sentence — was the WO accurate?), Discovery Log (what you checked, learned, rejected — even if efficient), Methodology Challenge (one thing to push back on), Field Manual Entry (one ready-to-paste tradecraft entry for BUILDER_FIELD_MANUAL.md). Do NOT edit BUILDER_FIELD_MANUAL.md directly — the PM curates it from your debrief. (10) pm_inbox HYGIENE: pm_inbox root has a 10-file cap. Your debrief will be archived to pm_inbox/reviewed/ after PM review. This is normal — it is not lost.
